title: 第 80 節
en: 5. The Devas (speech, &c.) said to him (the breath) : ' He is to be loved by all of us.' Because the Devas said of him, that he was to be loved (vama) by all of them, therefore there is (the poet of the fourth Mazztfala of the Rig-veda, called) Vamadeva. Therefore people call him who is really Prazza (breath), Vamadeva.

6. He (breath) guarded all this whatsoever from evil. Because he guarded (atrayata) all this whatso- 1 The real ground for the name is that the poets of the first Ma«(/ala composed on an average each about a hundred Rik verses.
2 I 6 AITAREYA-ARAiVYAKA.
ever from evil, therefore there are (the poets of the fifth Ma/^ala of the Rig-veda, called) Atraya/&. Therefore people call him who is really Pra^a (breath), Atraya/i Second Khanda.

i. He (breath) is likewise a Bibhradva^a (bringer of offspring). Offspring is va^a, and he (breath) supports offspring. Because he supports it, therefore there is (the poet of the sixth Mawakla of the Rig-veda, called) Bharadva^a. Therefore people call him who is really Pra^a (breath), Bharadva^a.

2. The Devas (speech, &c.) said to him : ' He it is who chiefly causes us to dwell on earth.' Because the Devas said of him, that he chiefly caused them to dwell on earth, therefore there is (the poet of the seventh Ma«flfala of the Rig-veda, called) Vasish///a. Therefore people call him who is really Praz^a (breath), Vasish//^a \ 3. He (breath) went forth towards 2 all this whatsoever. Because he went forth toward all this whatsoever, therefore there are (the poets of the eighth Ma«flfala of the Rig-veda, called) the Pragathas. Therefore people call him who is really Pra»a (breath), the Pragathas.

4. He (breath) purified all this whatsoever. Because he purified all this whatsoever, therefore there 1 I translate in accordance with the commentator, and probably with the intention of the author. The same etymology is repeated in the commentary on II, 2, 4, 2. It would be more natural to take vasish//;a in the sense of the richest.
This is the interpretation of the commentator, and the preposition abhi seems to show that the author too took that view of the etymology of pragatha.

II ARAiVYAKA, 2 ADHYAYA, 2 KHANDA, 8. 21 7 are (the hymns and also the poets x of the ninth Mandala. of the Rig-veda, called) the Pavamanis. Therefore people called him who is really Pra^a (breath), the Pavamanis.

5. He (breath) said : ' Let me be everything whatsoever, small (kshudra) and great (mahat), and this became the Kshudrasuktas and Mahasiiktas.' Therefore there were (the hymns and also the poets of the tenth Ma/^/ala of the Rig-veda, called) the Kshudrasuktas (and Mahasiiktas). Therefore people call him who is really Pra^a (breath), the Kshudrasuktas (and Mahasiiktas).

6. He (breath) said once : ' You have said what is well said (su-ukta) indeed. This became a Sukta (hymn).' Therefore there was the Sukta. Therefore people call him who is really Pra^a (breath), Sukta2.

7. He (breath) is a Rik (verse), for he did honour3 to all beings (by entering into them). Because he did honour to all beings, therefore there was the Rik verse. Therefore people call him who is really Pra^a (breath), Rik.

8. He (breath) is an Ardhar^a (half-verse), for he did honour to all places (ardha) 4. Because he did honour to all places, therefore there was the Ardhar/§a. Therefore people call him who is really Pra/za (breath), Ardhar^a.

1 It seems, indeed, as if in the technical language of the Brahmans, the poets of the ninth Ma;/</ala were sometimes called Pavamanis, and the hymns of the tenth Ma#dala Kshudrasuktas and Mahasiiktas (masc.) Cf. Arsheya-brahmawa, ed. Burnell, p. 42.
2 The poet also is called Sukta, taddrash/api suktanamako 'bhut. Comm.
3 I translate according to the commentator.
4 Ardha means both half and place.
2 I 8 AlTAREYA-ARAiVYAKA.

9. He (breath) is a Pada (word)1, for he got into all these beings. Because he got (padi) into all these beings, therefore there was the Pada (word). Therefore people call him who is really Pra;za (breath), Pada.

10. He (breath) is an Akshara (syllable), for he pours out (ksharati) gifts to all these beings, and without him no one can pour out (atiksharati) gifts. Therefore there was the Akshara (syllable). Therefore people call him who is really Pra/za (breath), Akshara 2.
11. Thus all these Rik verses, all Vedas, all sounds3 are one word, viz. Pra/za (breath). Let him know that Pra^a is all Rik verses.
Third Khajv.da.

1. While Visvamitra was going to repeat the hymns of this day (the mahavrata), Indra sat down near him4. Vi.rvamitra (guessing that Indra wanted food) said to him, ' This (the verses of the hymn) is food,' and repeated the thousand Br/hati verses5.
1 It may also be intended for pada, foot of a verse.
2 The Pra«a (breath) is to be meditated on as all hymns, all poets, all words, &c. Coram."
3 All aspirated sonant consonants. Comm.
4 Upanishasasada, instead of upanishasada. The mistake is probably due to a correction, sa for sha ; the commentator, however, considers it as a Vedic license. Sakaro 'dhikaj X'Mndasa/z.

5 These are meant for the Nishkevalya hymn recited at the noon-libation of the Mahavrata. That hymn consists of ten parts, corresponding, as we saw, to ten parts of a bird, viz. its body, neck, head, root of wings, right wing, left wing, tail, belly, chest, and thighs. The verses corresponding to these ten parts, beginning with tad id asa bhuvaneshu gyesh//iam, are given in the first Arawyaka, and more fully in the fifth Arawyaka by .Saunaka.
II ARAJVYAKA, 2 ADHYAYA, 3 KUANDA, 4. 219 By means of this he went to the delightful home of Indra (Svarga).

2. Indra said to him : ' Rishl, thou hast come to my delightful home. jRzshi, repeat a second hymn1.' Vi^vamitra (guessing that Indra wanted food) said to him, ' This (the verses of the hymn) is food,' and repeated the thousand Brz'hati verses. By means of this he went to the delightful home of Indra (Svarga).
zh: 5. 提婆（言語等）對他（呼吸）說：『他應為我們眾人所愛。』因為提婆說他應為（vama）他們眾人所愛，因此就有了（梨俱吠陀第四曼陀羅的詩人，稱為）跋摩提婆（Vamadeva）。因此人們稱呼那真實的普拉那（breath，呼吸）者為跋摩提婆。
6. 他（呼吸）守護了這一切，使其免受邪惡。因為他守護了（atrayata）這一切，1 這個名稱的真正根據在於第一曼陀羅（Ma«(/ala）的詩人們平均每人各作約百首梨克詩句。
216 愛他洛耶奧義書（AITAREYA-ARANYAKA）。
永遠免於邪惡，因此有（梨俱吠陀第五曼荼羅的詩人，稱為）阿特賴耶（Atrayah）。因此人們稱那真正是普拉那（Prana，呼吸）者為阿特賴耶（Atrayah）。第二分。
他（氣息）同樣是毗婆羅馱婆（Bibhradva^a，生育的帶來者）。生育是婆（va^a），而他（氣息）支撐生育。因為他支撐它，所以存在著（梨俱吠陀第六曼陀羅的詩人，稱為）跋羅陀婆（Bharadva^a）。因此人們稱呼那真實上是普拉那（Pra^a，氣息）的他為跋羅陀婆。

2. 提婆（言語等）對他說：「正是他使我們得以住在地上。」因為提婆說他主要使他們得以住在地上，因此有（梨俱吠陀第七曼荼羅的詩人，名為）婆濕笈多（Vasish//a）。因此人們稱呼真正是生氣（pra»a）的他為婆濕笈多。3. 他（生氣）向一切行進而去。因為他向一切行進而去，因此有（梨俱吠陀第八曼荼羅的詩人，名為）普拉伽他（the Pragathas）。因此人們稱呼真正是生氣（pra»a）的他為普拉伽他。
4. 他（氣息）淨化了所有一切。因為他淨化了所有一切，因此有之¹ ¹ 我按照註釋者的解釋，也可能按照作者的本意而翻譯。同樣的詞源解說在第二卷第二章第四節第二段的註釋中重複出現。將 vasishtha 理解為「最豐富的」可能更加自然。
這是評註者的詮釋，而前置詞 abhi 似乎表明作者本人也採取了對 pragatha 詞源的這種看法。
《阿蘭尼亞卡》（Aranyaka）第二學習篇（Adhyaya）第二章（Khanda）第8節。梨俱吠陀第九曼陀羅的詩歌及其詩人共七位，稱為《帕瓦曼尼詩》（Pavamanis）。因此人們將真正的普拉那（Prana，呼吸）稱為《帕瓦曼尼詩》。
他（呼吸）說：「讓我成為一切萬物，無論微小（kshudra）與宏大（mahat），而這便成為了《小讚歌》（Kshudrasuktas）與《大讚歌》（Mahasiiktas）。」因此便有了（梨俱吠陀第十卷的讚歌及其詩人，稱為）《小讚歌》（與《大讚歌》）。因此人們稱呼那真正是普拉那（呼吸）（Prana）的存在為《小讚歌》（與《大讚歌》）。
他（呼吸）曾經說過：「你確實說了美好的言詞（su-ukta）。這成為了一首頌歌（Sukta）。」因此便有了頌歌（Sukta）。因此人們稱那位真正的呼吸者（Prāṇa）為頌歌（Sukta）²。
他（呼吸）是一部梨俱（rik）[韻頌]，因為他對所有生靈表示尊敬（藉由進入他們之中）。因為他對所有生靈表示尊敬，所以才有了梨俱韻頌。因此人們稱真正的普拉那（prāṇa）[呼吸]者為梨俱。
他（呼吸）是一個阿陀婆（Ardhar^a）（半詩句），因為他尊敬了所有的地方（ardha）。因為他尊敬了所有的地方，所以才有了阿陀婆（Ardhar/§a）。因此人們稱呼那位真正是普拉那（Pra/za）（呼吸）的存在為阿陀婆。
似乎在婆羅門人的專業術語中，第九曼陀羅（Maṇḍala）的詩人有時被稱為帕瓦曼尼斯（Pavamanis），第十曼陀羅的讚歌則被稱為克蘇德拉蘇克塔斯（Kshudrasuktas）和摩訶蘇克塔斯（Mahasūktas）（陽性）。參見Arsheya-brāhmaṇa, ed. Burnell, p. 42。
詩人也被稱為蘇克塔（Sukta），如同註釋所云：「他也成為蘇克塔之名」（taddrash/api suktanamako 'bhut）。
我根據註疏者的解釋進行翻譯。
4 Ardha 既表示一半，亦表示地方。
218 阿依達雷亞奧義書（Aitareya-Aranyaka）
他（呼吸）是一個帕達（詞語）¹，因為他進入了所有這些眾生之中。由於他進入（padi）了所有這些眾生，因此才有了帕達（詞語）。因此人們稱呼真正的普拉那（呼吸）為帕達。
10. 他（氣息）是一個不變音節（Akshara），因為他向這一切眾生傾注（ksharati）禮物，沒有他誰也不能傾注（atiksharati）禮物。因此才有不變音節（Akshara）。因此人們稱那真正是風息（Pra/za）的他為不變音節。
11. 因此，所有梨俱頌、所有吠陀、所有聲音³都是一個詞，即普拉那（Pra/za，氣息）。讓他知道普拉那就是所有梨俱頌。
第三卷《梨俱吠陀》
當毗濕瓦彌多羅正要重誦此日的讚歌（摩訶禰陀）時，因陀羅坐在他身旁4。毗濕瓦彌多羅（猜測因陀羅想要食物）對他說：「此物（讚歌的詩句）即為食物」，並重誦了一千首勃里哈蒂詩句5。
它也許可以用於 pada（詩句的足部）。
2 普拉那（breath，呼吸）應當被冥想為一切讚歌、一切詩人、一切言辭等等。《歌者奧義書》（Coram.）
3 所有帶氣音的濁輔音。註。
4 《奧義書》本文作「upanishasasada」，而非「upanishasada」。此誤謬恐怕源於一處訂正，即「sa」代「sha」；然而評註者卻將其視為吠陀文獻的一種用法許可。Sakaro 'dhikaj X'Mndasa/z。

這些是為尼什凱瓦利亞讚歌（Nishkevalya hymn）而作，該讚歌在摩訶婆羅多祭（Mahavrata）的正午奠酒儀式上吟誦。如我們所見，該讚歌由十個部分組成，分別對應鳥的十個部分，即其身體、頸部、頭部、翅膀根部、右翅、左翅、尾部、腹部、胸部與大腿。對應這十個部分的詩節，始於「tad id asa bhuvaneshu gyesh//iam」，見載於第一阿蘭耶迦書（Arawyaka），並由舍由迦（Saunaka）在第五阿蘭耶迦書中更為詳盡地記述。
第二章《阿蘭尼亞卡》第二次講述第三部分第219節。藉由此道，他來到了因陀羅（Svarga）令人喜悅的家園。
2. 因陀羅對他說：「仙人啊，你已來到我喜樂之家。仙人啊，再誦一首讚歌吧。」毗濕瓦密多羅（猜測因陀羅想要食物）對他說：「這（讚歌的詩句）就是食物，」並誦唸了千首布里哈蒂詩句。藉由此法，他來到了因陀羅的喜樂之家（天界 Svarga）。
